violations.text: During inspection, there was no on-site manager certified in food safety while time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (bun) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. PIC discard bun. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took unit out of service to be washed/sanitized. PIC disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200-400 ppm. PIC changed out sanitizer bottle, but equipment is not pulling enough sanitizer. Establishment to wash/sanitize via dishwasher or manually add sanitizer to 3 bay sink until unit is serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Inspector observed containers of flour and water used in contact with raw chicken stored at room temperature. Per interview with staff, flour and water are changed after 6 hours. Staff informed to change out containers every 4 hours. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed parcooked chicken wings cooling in the walk-in refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC changed to shallower container for cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The doors to the outside, located in the (kitchen and front), are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026
Containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The cutting board at the prep unit is very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quat sanitizing solution for the dishwasher and 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the all refrigeration and freezer units have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The ice/soda fountain drainage was observed draining into a container. Nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2026
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026
The back of establishment (outside) was observed with debris and equipment (soda racks). Area to be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
Utensils were observed stored in sanitizer between uses. PIC informed of proper storage Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of (food product ). Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024

The cutting boards in the (kitchen) are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
The stove hoods have an accumulation of dust and soil residue. New Violation. Correct By: 11/21/2024
